location.office_time_required: within commuting distance of hub; in-person time for planning, problem-solving, and connection

level_signals: Owns product strategy, roadmap, and execution
Balancing short and long-term goals at platform scale
Operates across ambiguity and drives execution across multiple teams

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 7+ years of product management experience, with a track record of owning product strategy, roadmap, and execution while balancing short and long-term goals.
Strong product judgment with the ability to balance buyer experience, seller needs, and business economics
Experience building or scaling complex systems that combine user experience with underlying platform or infrastructure
A data-driven mindset with experience defining metrics, running experiments, and using insights to drive decisions
Ability to operate across ambiguity, identify high-leverage opportunities, and drive execution across multiple teams
Excellent cross-functional collaboration skills, with experience partnering closely with Engineering, Design, and Operations

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience with shipping, fulfillment, or multi-sided marketplaces with logistics components
Experience in a marketplace, logistics, or systems-heavy environment

scope.surface: Logistics — shipping systems, carrier integrations, pricing/bundling, fulfillment tools, and logistics platform infrastructure across global marketplace
scope.business_model: B2C/B2B marketplace (live commerce / livestream shopping platform), multi-sided
core_responsibilities: Ensure that anything can be sold and fulfilled on Whatnot by identifying shipping options that unlock new categories, countries, and seller segments
Accomplish best-in-class shipping reliability as measured by on-time scan rates and fulfillment standards
Improve shipping affordability and transparency by designing pricing, bundling, and carrier solutions
Drive sustainable logistics economics by building scalable infrastructure, data systems, and user experiences
Partner with Engineering, Design, Data Science, Operations, and external partners to deliver user-facing and platform logistics experiences

green_flags: Clear product surface with specific measurable outcomes (on-time scan rates, fulfillment standards, shipping affordability)
Role sits at the center of the platform — high business-critical visibility
Explicit hub cities listed for hybrid arrangement — no ambiguity about location requirement
Strong benefits package including 401k match, parental leave, and dogfooding budget
Named #1 Best Startup Employer in America by Forbes — employer brand signal
Encourages candidates without every qualification through EOE and culture section
red_flags: Visa sponsorship not mentioned — unclear for international candidates
Hybrid policy requires commuting distance to SF, Seattle, LA, or NYC — eliminates fully remote candidates outside those metros
No team size or org structure details provided

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 7+ years requirement and scope of ownership)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not listed as explicit KPIs)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description as multi-sided live commerce marketplace)
ambiguities: JD describes the team as 'remote co-located' with global hubs, but then specifies team members must live within commuting distance of SF, Seattle, LA, or NYC — treated as hybrid/onsite-required rather than remote-eligible.
No compensation range disclosed anywhere in the JD — source marked as unknown.
